Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Nanette J. Burgos Aka Nanette Burgos And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF KATSIARYNA YAZERSKAYA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Nanette J. Burgos Aka Nanette Burgos And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Katsiaryna Yzerskaya's motion to compel further responses to her first set of form interrogatories and requests for production, and for sanctions, is granted in part and denied in part.

Form interrogatories: Defendant Nanette Burgos's response to 12.1 is incomplete. Identifying one witness whom defendant believes has the most knowledge is not a complete and straightforward response to the request to identify "each individual."

For form interrogatories 15.1 through 16.10, Yzerskaya cannot seek further responses because her motion comes past the 45-day jurisdictional limit. A responding party's vague promise to amend does not toll the running of the time to move to compel; it is incumbent upon the propounding party to set deadlines for amendment or obtain extensions of the time to move to compel.

Requests for production: For RFP Nos. 1-4, 8, 10, 12, 20, 22, 23, 26: Defendant's statement of compliance represents that documents will be produced in full, which means that no responsive documents have been withheld from the production. Defendant belatedly produced the documents after Plaintiff reminded Defendant to do so. The court concludes that Defendant's delay was unreasonable. For these RFPs, the court does not order further production but concludes that the Plaintiff's motion resulted in the production, a factor in the sanctions analysis.

RFP Nos. 5, 14, 16, 19, 24: Defendant's response states that "responsive documents have never existed." Plaintiff's motion includes some documents responsive to nos. 5 and 14. This suggests that either defendant's search was incomplete or its response was inaccurate. Defendant is ordered to provide plaintiff with a verified description of its search parameters and search efforts for these RFPs within 10 days of entry of this order and to further meet and confer about whether its search was sufficient. Defendant shall amend within 25 days of entry of this order if after meet and confer efforts it concludes that its response was inaccurate or its search was incomplete. Plaintiff may bring a renewed motion to compel based on either the verified descriptions or the amended responses if any.

RFP No. 6: Defendant's objections are overruled. To the extent Defendant asserts affirmative defenses, it must produce any supporting documents, even if it has not yet located all such documents. Defendant shall amend its response within 10 days of entry of this order and produce all nonprivileged responsive documents within 25 days of entry of this order.

RFP Nos. 11, 17: Defendant's response is sufficient and no further response is ordered.

The relevant discovery statutes state that the court shall award sanctions against a party who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel further responses unless the party acted with substantial justification. Here, Defendant has largely unsuccessfully opposed the motion to compel and the court does not find substantial justification. The court awards partial sanctions of $750, payable by Defendant to Plaintiff. Defendant shall pay the sanctions within 25 days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
